name: Pyxis Oncology, Inc.
business: Pyxis Oncology is a clinical-stage oncology company advancing a development strategy focused on addressing unmet medical needs in patients with solid tumors with an immediate focus on head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC). Its lead product candidate, micvotabart pelidotin (MICVO), is an investigational novel antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) that uniquely targets the splice variant of fibronectin, extradomain-B of fibronectin (EDB+FN), a non-cellular structural component of the extracellular matrix (ECM) in the tumor microenvironment (TME).
sellsTo: patients with recurrent/metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (R/M HNSCC)

exposures.sentence: Pyxis Oncology is a clinical-stage oncology company advancing a development strategy focused on addressing unmet medical needs in patients with solid tumors with an immediate focus on head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC).

catalysts.event: Expected reporting of updated clinical data from the monotherapy dose expansion cohort (Arm 1) in 2L+ R/M HNSCC
catalysts.sentence: We expect to enroll approximately 20 patients in this expansion cohort at the 5.4 mg/kg IV Q3W dose and anticipate reporting updated clinical data from this cohort in mid-2026.

catalysts.event: Expected reporting of updated clinical data from the monotherapy dose expansion cohort (Arm 2) in 2L+ R/M HNSCC
catalysts.sentence: We expect to enroll approximately 20 patients in this expansion cohort at the 5.4 mg/kg IV Q3W dose and anticipate reporting updated clinical data from this cohort in mid-2026.

catalysts.event: Expected reporting of updated Phase 1 monotherapy data in 2L+ R/M HNSCC, including modified weight-based dosing strategy analysis
catalysts.sentence: MICVO Phase 1 monotherapy data in 2L+ R/M HNSCC expected in mid-year 2026 will include patients dosed at 5.4 mg/kg IV Q3W with a dose cap for patients with higher body weight, as well as patients previously dosed at 5.4 mg/kg IV Q3W based on total body weight.

catalysts.event: Expected reporting of updated data from the PYX-201-102 combination study (MICVO + pembrolizumab) in 1L/2L+ R/M HNSCC
catalysts.sentence: We expect to report updated data from the PYX-201-102 study in patients with 1L/2L+ R/M HNSCC in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Completed target enrollment of approximately 40 patients in Phase 1 monotherapy dose expansion study in 2L+ R/M HNSCC
catalysts.sentence: We completed target enrollment of approximately 40 patients in the Phase 1 monotherapy dose expansion study of MICVO in 2L+ R/M HNSCC in the first quarter of 2026 and are actively treating patients in two monotherapy R/M HNSCC cohorts at the 5.4 mg/kg IV Q3W dose.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately one million new cases of HNSCC worldwide annually by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that by 2030 there will be approximately one million new cases of HNSCC worldwide annually per 2022 GLOBOCAN estimates.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 60,000 cases of HNSCC each year in the U.S. with a 13% 5-year survival rate in the R/M setting
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 60,000 cases of HNSCC each year in the U.S. with a 13% 5-year survival rate in the R/M (Stage IVC) setting.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 31,000 cases of R/M HNSCC each year in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 31,000 cases of R/M HNSCC each year in the U.S. with ~67% (~21,000) of patients progressing from 1L to 2L and facing a sharp decline in survival.

tamClaims.claim: Head and neck cancer is the seventh most common cancer globally with 940,000 new cases and 480,000 deaths per year
tamClaims.sentence: Head and Neck Cancer (HNC) is the seventh most common cancer in the world (Source: NIH), with 940,000 new cases and 480,000 deaths from HNC per 2022 GLOBOCAN estimates.
